data.title: Nasdaq notifies Cambium Networks on securities subject to delisting
data.description:
Cambium Networks (NASDAQ:CMBM) announced on Thursday the receipt of a staff determination letter from Nasdaq stating that the networking solutions provider's securities are now subject to delisting.

The company had received a deficiency letter from the stock exchange on April 10, stating that for 30 consecutive business days, the bid price for the company's ordinary shares closed below the $1.00 per share requirement for continued inclusion.

As the company did not regain compliance by October 7, the shares are now subject to delisting.

The stock exchange said the letter will not immediately result in the suspension of trading or delisting of the securities.

Additionally, Cambium Networks received a deficiency letter from the stock exchange on non-compliance with timely filing of the annual report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024, and quarterly reports on Forms 10-Q for the periods ended March 31, 2025, and June 30, 2025.

On September 9, the company notified the stock exchange of its inability to regain compliance with the rule by the required period.

data.title: atai Life Sciences, Beckley Psytech get FDA breakthrough therapy status for BPL-003
data.description:
• atai Life Sciences (NASDAQ:ATAI) and Beckley Psytech have received Breakthrough Therapy designation from the FDA for their nasal spray BPL-003 (mebufotenin benzoate) aimed at adults suffering from treatment-resistant depression.
  • The breakthrough therapy status is intended to speed up the development of medications that address serious or life-threatening conditions.
  • This designation comes after promising results from a phase 2b study conducted by Beckley Psytech.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: CSX Corp beats top and bottom line in third quarter
CSX rallies after leading off the earnings season for the rails sector
data.description:
CSX Corporation (NASDAQ:CSX) traded higher in postmarket action after it led off the earnings season for the rails sector.

Revenue fell 0.8% year-over-year to $3.59 billion, topping the consensus estimate by $20 million. The effects of lower export coal prices and a decline in merchandise volume were only partially offset by increases in other revenue, higher pricing in merchandise, and intermodal volume growth.

Volume totaled 1.61 million units for the quarter, up 1% year-over-year and 2% higher sequentially. Volume was higher in the minerals (+8%), automotive (+1%), and fertilizers (+7)  categories, while lower in the chemicals (-7%), forest products (-7%), and agricultural and food products (-7%) categories.

CSX (NASDAQ:CSX) reported adjusted operating income of $1.25 billion and an operating margin of 34.9%. Non-GAAP EPS of $0.44 for the quarter was ahead of the consensus estimate of $0.42 but trailed last year's mark of $0.46.

"This quarter’s operational performance reflects the dedication of our workforce and our commitment to running the best railroad in North America," stated CEO Steve Angel. "We see clear opportunities to leverage that operational strength moving forward. Looking ahead, CSX is well-positioned to build on this momentum to deliver long-term profitable growth and create value for our shareholders," he added.

Shares of CSX (CSX) rose 3.3% in postmarket trading. The rails stock is up more than 10% over the last six weeks amid the M&A focus in the sector. Union Pacific (UNP) and Norfolk Southern (NSC) have agreed to merge to form the nation's first coast-to-coast transcontinental freight rail operator, pending regulatory approval. The deal is valued at approximately $85 billion and is expected to undergo a lengthy review process by the federal Surface Transportation Board.

data.title: Bank OZK GAAP EPS of $1.59 misses by $0.10, revenue of $449.93M beats by $3.07M
data.description:
• Bank OZK press release (NASDAQ:OZK): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.59 misses by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $449.93M (+6.4% Y/Y) beats by $3.07M.
  • Shares -3.02% AH.

data.title: TeraWulf prices $3.2B offering of senior secured notes
data.description:
• TeraWulf (NASDAQ:WULF) on Thursday said its unit WULF Compute LLC priced a $3.2 billion offering of 7.75% senior secured notes due 2030 at par.

  • The private placement, expected to close on Oct. 23, will be sold to qualified institutional buyers under Rule 144A.

  • The company said proceeds will fund part of a data center expansion at its Lake Mariner campus in Barker, New York.
  • The notes will be guaranteed by several subsidiaries and secured by first-priority liens on substantially all assets of WULF Compute and its guarantors.
  • WULF +0.54% after hours to $13.93.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Newsmax announces crypto plan for asset reserve
data.description:
• Newsmax (NYSE:NMAX) said on Thursday that its board authorized a plan to purchase up to $5 million worth of Bitcoin and Trump Coin over the next 12 months, depending on market conditions.
  • Newsmax said it plans to make the first tranche of purchases soon and would be the first NYSE-listed company to hold Trump Coin.
  • Future acquisitions will depend on market conditions and operational needs.
  • NMAX +2.75% after hours to $11.17.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Glacier Bancorp GAAP EPS of $0.57 misses by $0.04, revenue of $260.73M misses by $0.28M
data.description:
• Glacier Bancorp press release (NYSE:GBCI): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.57 misses by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $260.73M (+8.4% Y/Y) misses by $0.28M.
  • Shares -1.55% AH.

data.title: Praxis Precision Medicines announces proposed public offering
data.description:
• Praxis Precision Medicines (NASDAQ:PRAX) said on Thursday it plans to offer shares of its common stock and prefunded warrants in a public offering, with all securities to be sold by the company.
  • The size and pricing of the offering were not disclosed.
  • The company granted underwriters a 30-day option to buy up to 15% additional shares.
  • PRAX -2.92% after hours to $157.78.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Wall Street slides on trade war concerns, bank loans reports, ongoing U.S. shutdown
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages closed lower on Thursday amid increased volatility due to the ongoing U.S.-China trade conflict, and bad loans reports in banks, while investors continued to assess earnings reports and the ongoing U.S. government shutdown.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) closed -0.6%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) finished -0.5%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) ended -0.7%. 

"Today was a great example of just how unfavorable the risk/reward for the market has become. Trading at 23.0x forward earnings, the market knows that any negative headlines could cause a sell-off due to the high dependence on elevated future growth,” Seeking Alpha analyst Leo Nelissen said.

Bank stocks took a dive after Zions Bancorporation (ZION) -13.2% and Western Alliance (WAL) -11.1% disclosed irregularities related to loans.

“While I do not believe today’s banking news is the start of any systematic weakness, we need to get used to elevated volatility on days when the focus isn’t on AI or other favorable developments," Nelissen said.

On earnings, companies such as Charles Schwab (SCHW) and U.S. Bancorp (USB) saw shares initially rise after earnings beat and earnings growth, but both closed lower at -1% and -1.8%, respectively.

In other tech news, Salesforce (CRM) +3.6% and Google (GOOGL) (GOOG) announced an expansion of their existing partnership on Thursday to bring Gemini AI models to the former Agentforce 360 Platform.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 5 basis points to 3.98% -- its lowest level since April -- while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was 8 basis point lower at 3.43%.

The U.S. government shutdown is now entering its third week, as a GOP funding bill fails for the 10th time. Prediction market Kalshi now sees the government shutdown lasting more than 39 days.

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said that China's lead trade negotiator Li Chenggang acted "unhinged" in Washington and warned that China would retaliate if the U.S. imposed port fees on its ships.

Bessent had earlier said that President Donald Trump "is a go" for a meeting with Xi Jinping and that he was positive about the ongoing discussions with China. 

In economic news, the NAHB/Wells Fargo Housing Market Index rose to 37 in October from 32 a month before, vs. 33 consensus.

U.S. stocks ended mixed after a choppy session on Wednesday, with the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) gaining ground as upbeat earnings reports eclipsed U.S.-China trade tension worries.
